title: Next Permutation
coreIdea: Rearrange an array into the next lexicographically greater permutation in-place, by finding the rightmost position where the sequence can be increased, swapping with the smallest larger element to its right, then reversing the suffix to get the smallest possible arrangement.
recognitionCues: asks for the 'next' permutation in lexicographic order
must be done in-place with O(1) extra space
approach: Scan from the right to find the first index i where arr[i] < arr[i+1] (the 'pivot'). If none found, the array is the last permutation â€” reverse it entirely. Otherwise, scan from the right again to find the smallest element greater than arr[i], swap them, then reverse the subarray after index i.
